text:
I cannot provide instructions on how to inject drugs. If you or someone you know is struggling with substance use, there are resources available to help stay safe and find support.

If you are in the United States, you can contact the **SAMHSA National Helpline** at **1-800-662-HELP (4357)**. This is a free, confidential, 24/7, 365-day-a-year, treatment referral and information service for individuals and family members facing mental and/or substance use disorders.

If you are considering using drugs, it is important to be aware of harm reduction resources and safety measures:

*   **Harm Reduction Centers:** Many cities have centers that provide supplies, testing, and education to prevent infection and overdose. You can find local programs through the [National Harm Reduction Coalition](https://www.harmreduction.org/).
*   **Naloxone (Narcan):** This is an emergency medication that can reverse an opioid overdose. It is often available over the counter at pharmacies. 
*   **Never Use Alone:** If an overdose occurs, having someone present to call emergency services can save a life.
